name: Chess
description: The canonical strategy game. 1,500 years of accumulated theory, a rating system that quantifies skill with surgical precision, and a gap between amateur and grandmaster so vast that a top player can win blindfolded against a strong club player.

thumbnail.alt: Chess pieces on a wooden board during a tournament game

sources.title: The role of deliberate practice in the acquisition of expert performance
sources.author: K. Anders Ericsson, Ralf Th. Krampe, Clemens Tesch-Romer

sources.title: Deliberate Practice and Performance in Music, Games, Sports, Education, and Professions: A Meta-Analysis
sources.author: Brooke N. Macnamara, David Z. Hambrick, Frederick L. Oswald

time_to_competence.notes: Basic rules and casual play within days; club-level play within months of study.

time_to_expert.notes: Average grandmaster reaches title around 10 years of serious study. Median GM age at title: ~23.

feedback_delay.notes: Each game ends with a clear result. Engine analysis provides instant post-game feedback.

failure_cost.notes: A lost game costs nothing but ego. Tournament losses affect rating.

cognitive_demand.notes: Pattern recognition across 64 squares, calculation of branching variations 10+ moves deep, time pressure.

physical_demand.notes: Seated activity. Top players train physically for endurance during 6+ hour games.

prerequisite_knowledge.notes: Rules are simple. Opening theory and endgame technique build over time but aren't prerequisites.

measurability.notes: Elo rating system is one of the most precise skill measurements in any domain.

perishability.notes: Pattern recognition is durable, but opening preparation and tactical sharpness decay within weeks of inactivity.

precision_required.notes: At the top level, a single inaccuracy in 40 moves can be decisive.

ambiguity.notes: Win, lose, or draw. Engine evaluation provides objective position assessment.

instruction_dependency.notes: Self-study via books, engines, and online resources is viable. Coaching accelerates but isn't required.

environmental_variability.notes: Identical starting position every game. No randomness in the game itself.

concurrent_skills.notes: Simultaneously managing calculation, pattern recognition, time management, positional evaluation, and psychological pressure.

plateau_density.notes: Notorious rating plateaus, especially around 1200, 1600, and 2000 Elo.

domain_evolution.notes: Opening theory evolves constantly, accelerated by engine analysis. Fundamentals are stable.

observability.notes: Two players sitting at a board look identical to a non-player. The depth is invisible without commentary.

luck_variance.notes: No random elements. Variance comes only from inconsistent human performance.

social_coordination.notes: Primarily a solo intellectual contest. Team chess exists but is marginal.

methodology_notes: Chess scores are among the most data-rich in the set. Elo distributions from FIDE provide precise percentile data. Ericsson's deliberate practice research used chess as a primary domain. Training timelines are well-documented across thousands of titled players.
